member.name: Gregor Poynton

addresses: Craigsfarm Community Hub, Maree Walk, Craigshill, Livingston, EH54 5BP

member.synopsis: Gregor Poynton is the Labour MP for Livingston , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently holds the Government post of Assistant Whip.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Manufacturing Industries: Trade Fairs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to support British manufacturers to attend (a) Marmomac in Verona and (b) other major international trade shows.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through our Trade Strategy, Industrial Strategy, and Small Business Plan, we're putting in place the policies, support, and services needed to drive export-led growth, particularly in high-growth potential sectors like advanced manufacturing.We routinely ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.answerText: Though our Trade Strategy, Industrial Strategy, and Small Business Plan, we are putting in place the policies, support, and services needed to drive export-led business growth.We continue to review how we can improve our support, and as part of this proce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps her Department is taking to ensure the effective enforcement of The Product Safety and Metrology etc. (Amendment) Regulations 2024 in relation to imported construction
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Product Safety and Metrology etc. (Amendment) Regulations provide for continued recognition of certain EU requirements for a range of products, but do not apply to construction products.The Construction Products Regulation 2011 (as amended) governs th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is currently consulting on introducing minimum energy efficiency standards for the social rented sector. We will be engaging closely with the sector throughout the consultation process. The Warm Homes: Social Housing Fund provides grant fun...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Rented Housing: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of modern, low-disruption retrofit techniques on the rate of decarbonisation of the UK’s social housing stock.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department places significant importance on the tenants needs, and complying with consumer protection standards when completing retrofit works. The Department takes an iterative approach to schemes - such as increasing the provision of modern low carb...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Heating: Social Rented Housing

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has committed £13.2 billion over the Spending Review period for the Warm Homes Plan, which will support investment in insulation and low carbon heating to homes across England. Within Wave 3 of the Warm Homes: Social Housing Fund, which lau...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.answerText: Through our Trade Strategy, Industrial Strategy, and Small Business Plan, we are putting in place the policies, support, and services needed to drive export-led growth. For the first time, DBT is integrating its support for SMEs across all sectors in a si...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of supporting access to second-hand battery electric vehicles through reduced benefit-in-kind taxation on used electric cars.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is fully committed to the transition to electric vehicles and a strong second-hand market for EVs plays an important role in this. The Company Car Tax regime helps support the used electric vehicle markets, where electric company cars are s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if she will take steps to ensure that imported construction products are subject to the same UKCA marking requirements as those manufactured in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Construction products supplied to the GB market must be affixed with a UKCA or CE mark where they are covered by a designated standard under the UK Construction Product Regulations or where they conform to a UK technical assessment which has been issued f...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles: Secondhand Goods
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of introducing targeted funding support for the purchase of used battery electric vehicles; and what steps her Department is taking to ensure parity of finan
writtenQuestions.answerText: We continue to work with industry to support a sustainable second-hand zero emission vehicle market. The Government expects that the Electric Car Grant (ECG) will have some benefit in the used market due to a higher supply of affordable vehicles in future...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Imports: Certification
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department has undertaken a recent review of the effectiveness of enforcement mechanisms for CE marking of imported products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We keep the effectiveness of our enforcement regime under review. The Product Regulation and Metrology Act 2025, received Royal Assent in July and contains powers to strengthen product enforcement.The Office for Product Standards and Safety (OPSS) is the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether his Department plans to require AI developers to demonstrate that their models include technical protections against the generation of (a) child sexual abuse imagery and (b) oth
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of tackling AI-generated child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Creating, possessing, or distributing CSAM, including AI Generated CSAM, is illegal. The Online Safety Act requires services to proactively identify and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Child Sexual Abuse Material
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what recent assessment his Department has made of the risk of generative AI being used to produce child sexual abuse material.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of tackling AI-generated child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Creating, possessing, or distributing CSAM, including AI Generated CSAM, is illegal. The Online Safety Act requires services to proactively identify and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Child Sexual Abuse Material
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether his Department plans to include statutory obligations for AI developers to adopt safety-by-design measures to prevent the creation of child sexual abuse content.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of tackling AI-generated child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Creating, possessing, or distributing CSAM, including AI Generated CSAM, is illegal. The Online Safety Act requires services to proactively identify and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Child Sexual Abuse Material
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that AI models are developed with safeguards to prevent their use in generating child sexual abuse material.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of tackling AI-generated child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Creating, possessing, or distributing CSAM, including AI Generated CSAM, is illegal. The Online Safety Act requires services to proactively identify and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Industry: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the Industrial Strategy on Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our modern Industrial Strategy will drive growth in Scotland's globally competitive sectors – from advanced manufacturing in Glasgow and the UK’s second-largest financial services sector in Edinburgh, to life sciences in Dundee and Aberdeen’s status as a ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of consumer flexibility on reducing energy costs; and what steps his Department is taking to ensure that clean energy produced in Scotland
writtenQuestions.answerText: Consumer led flexibility reduces electricity system costs for all by minimising the amount of peaking generation and associated network infrastructure that needs to be built in the long term. Modelling shows that deploying short duration flexibility such ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Clean Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his policies of the report by the Association for Decentralised Energy entitled Consumer-Led Clean Power: How to Unlock Consumer-
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government supports significant growth in consumer-led flexibility, as set out in the Clean Power 2030 Action Plan. We are grateful to the Association for Decentralised Energy for its work in this area. In our Action Plan, Government committed to pub...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: National Grid: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to accelerate grid connection times for small and medium-sized enterprises and manufacturers participating in grid-balancing schemes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Energy System Operator (NESO) is implementing fundamental reforms to the connections process that will free up capacity and accelerate connections, including for small and medium-sized enterprises and manufacturers. But we recognise that furt...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Billing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of e-invoicing for small businesses on reducing payment times.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to tackling late payments, which can be a major obstacle for small businesses. In September 2024 we announced new measures including a new Fair Payment Code (launched in December 2024), legislation requiring large companies to ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade
